key_insight: Random sampling maps provide a unified way to compare and compress inputs of different sizes, enabling both cross-size generalization and scalable sketching.
engineering_relevance: This gives practical guidance for building and evaluating models on variable-size data such as sets, sequences, graphs, and tensors when full-size inference is expensive.
method: Define sampling operators that relate large and small instances via symmetries of the domain.
Characterize which sampling scheme fits each domain: sampling with replacement, random binning, or species sampling.
Derive explicit generalization and sketching rates for functions continuous under the chosen sampling notion.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical characterization and rate results across broad function classes, but no empirical experiments or benchmark data.
watch_item: The framework’s guarantees depend on matching the sampling model to the domain’s symmetries, so a poor choice of sampling scheme may weaken the bounds or utility.
